currentFocus: State batons are slimmed to current-state pointers after the post-wave closeout and PatchBench static guard integration; the next developer slice remains a WinUI validation-wrapper short-path guard/docs follow-up.

publicMain.nextWriteRequirement: Before any later write, re-read live HEAD, origin/main, and remote refs/heads/main; exact hashes in state are accepted evidence, not standing future main truth.

acceptedWorkerCommits.summary: Reconciled goal and state batons for the previous modularity wave, removed stale pending-push/local-campaign wording, and preserved runtime/release/non-payload boundaries.
Hardened a static WinUI guard so literal PatchBench/Windowed & Mods button labels cannot expose Host/Join, matchmaking, or online action wording before required proof and promotion gates exist.

currentTruth.patchBenchBoundary: The integrated PatchBench change is test-only. AppCore receipt construction, patch planning, safe-copy manifests/signatures, launch arguments, process decisions, patch catalog rows, byte data, music behavior, Host/Join availability, online readiness, runtime proof, and release packaging are unchanged.
currentTruth.validationWrapperFinding: The Windows App SDK/XAML compiler failure observed in a deep campaign worktree is classified as a long-path/tooling issue. The next bounded slice should harden or document the WinUI primary-lane wrapper short-path behavior without changing product code or package scripts.
currentTruth.publicPrimaryRepo: The public repo remains the primary collaboration repo for source, docs, tools, tests, RE notes, wave notes, state batons, agent reports, readiness notes, and compact proof summaries that are non-secret and non-payload.
currentTruth.releasePublicationStatus: No release, tag, package, signing, installer, announcement, hosted workflow, or publication action is part of this integration.
currentTruth.stateBatonShape: State files should stay concise current-state handoffs. Completed release-audit history, repeated command logs, and local campaign details belong in canonical docs/readiness notes or local reports, not live state.

runtimeBoundaries.onlineMultiplayer: not player-ready; Host/Join remains disabled until required distinct-endpoint and source-bound copied-runtime proof exists
runtimeBoundaries.staticReClosure: static closure is not runtime, gameplay, visual, online, rebuild, or parity proof

currentTruth.hardPayloadBoundary: Hard payloads, screenshots/frame dumps, raw proof logs, copied executables, full Ghidra databases, secrets, .env* files, build output, package output, and local runtime caches stay out of git and release ZIPs; use LOCAL_LAB_OVERLAY.md for local-only placement.

activeSlice.name: winui-validation-wrapper-short-path-guard-docs
activeSlice.goal: Add a small tooling/docs guard for the WinUI primary-lane validation wrapper so intentional short invocation roots are preserved or long-path expansion is diagnosed clearly.
activeSlice.claimBoundary: future slice is tooling/docs-only unless separately authorized; no package-script, product behavior, release, Host/Join, online, runtime, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, original-BEA, patch-row, byte-patch, launch, safe-copy manifest/signature, music, gameplay, visual, rebuild, or no-noticeable-difference claim changes

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

validation.currentIntegration: Integration gates cover docs/repo safety, JSON parse, stale-path scan, WinUI build, focused and broader PatchBench tests, patch-engine safety, and primary-lane validation.
validation.nextSlice: Prefer a small WinUI validation-wrapper short-path guard/docs follow-up; keep it tooling/docs-only unless a separate product slice is authorized.

nextSteps: Before the next write, verify live HEAD, origin/main, and remote refs/heads/main.
Use the next slice for a small WinUI validation-wrapper short-path guard/docs follow-up.

Keep package scripts, release publication, Host/Join, online readiness, runtime audio, gameplay, visual, rebuild, no-noticeable-difference, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, and original-BEA mutation claims bounded to accepted evidence.
